stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is between 67% and 78% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is 79%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is 94%. What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.498 to 0.579 as the code review pass rate varies from 67% to 78%. Point estimate: 0.542.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the code review stage creates a 8.1 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the code review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
